claims.assertion: Position-command datasets have jerk spike rate below 2%
claims.conditions: spike defined as per-step jerk norm > 5x median jerk for that episode

falsification.condition: Any position-command hardware dataset with rate > 0.04

pending_tests.reason: Real hardware — spike rate may increase due to sensor noise and actuation lags

claims.assertion: Velocity-command datasets have jerk spike rate in the range 2–8% due to frequent direction reversals
claims.conditions: spike defined as per-step jerk norm > 5x median jerk for that episode

falsification.condition: A second velocity-command dataset with rate < 0.01 or > 0.12

pending_tests.reason: Single data point — cannot distinguish class property from pusht artifact

claims.assertion: Jerk spike rate is more robust to cross-frequency comparison than LDLJ, because the 5x-median threshold self-adapts to episode scale
claims.conditions: requires same spike_k parameter across datasets being compared

evidence.notes: Direction of difference matches physical expectation despite 5x frequency difference — tentatively supports frequency-robustness claim

falsification.condition: Two datasets of same control mode with 3x+ frequency difference showing inverted spike rates

pending_tests.dataset: A 10Hz position-command dataset vs aloha at 50Hz
pending_tests.reason: Would test whether frequency independence holds within a single control mode
